rationale: This is a textbook sdclt.exe UAC bypass attack chain. EV-001 and EV-002 show PowerShell writing a malicious command and a DelegateExecute value to HKU\...\Folder\shell\open\command, the well-known registry hijack path exploited by sdclt.exe to auto-elevate. A0 confirms sdclt.exe was then launched (from cmd.exe) at High integrity level, which triggered EV-003 (control.exe spawned by sdclt.exe at High integrity), which in turn spawned EV-004: a PowerShell process at High integrity executing a steganographic payload loader that reads pixel data from 'monkey.png' and invokes it via IEX — a clear attempt to execute hidden shellcode/payload with elevated privileges. EV-005 shows the registry key being deleted afterward, consistent with cleanup to reduce forensic footprint. The combination of registry-based UAC bypass, steganographic payload delivery, execution policy bypass (-ep bypass), hidden window, and post-execution cleanup leaves no credible benign explanation.
